events.comment:
at the beginning of the scene, we create the light objects
and we create and start the fade Transition object (using transition extension)

events.name: Check if player have saved data or not

events.comment:
We check if the player dont have any saved data into storage
then we set the continue button opacity to 70 (transparent)
we load all data related to player controls and we store each data into its own variable
if the number of instances of rain object is less than 175 (you can increase/decrease this number to increase the number of rain on the screen)

then we create rain object at random position on X and Y related to camera Borders
we repeat the event 3 times, to make the rain spawn faster (3 times per frame)

for each instance of rain we cast a ray toward the angle 90 (to bottom) at 20px (because the rain object height is 20) against the objects to raycast against (level and player)
so if the raycast got interupted with any of this objects, we store the Y position into the variable FloorYPosition
then we change the Rain object Y position set to that variable - 20 (means we push the rain object to the surface of that object)
and then we change its animation to Splash

If the rain top is greater or equal the camera border bottom
or
if the rain aniamtion splash has finished
then we change the rain object position to the top and at random X position
then we set the rain aniamtion back to still
(so rather than deleting the rain object when the splash animation has finished, or the rain object is out of screen bottom, we change its position back to the top, this is better for performance, since create/delete object eats some performance)
for each instance of rain, we simply add a force at 90 angle (to bottom) at 450 pixels speed

at the beginning of the scene
we disable the Brightness layer effect
and we set the cooldown variable to randomflaot in range between 5 and 20 (used to make the lightning happen at random time between 5-20 seconds)
then we start the lightning timer

if the timer (we started in the evnet above) is greater or equal to the variable (we set in the event above)
then we set the variable to random again
and we set the Lightning style variable to random between 1 and 2 (this variable is used to make 2 style of lightnings, each with its sound effect and flash style)
then we reset the timer

if the variable (set from the event above) was = 1
then we turn on the Brightness effect
and we set the light layer to complete white
and then we reset them abckj to default values after 0.1 seconds (flash duration)
same as above but different flash durations

if left mouse button is pressed
and none of the buttons animation is Pressed (to avoid player pressong on more than 1 button in the same time)
and no transitions instances are in the scene (to avoid the player clicking on a button while a transition is happening)
and the cursor is on continue button
and the opacity of the button is 255 (not transparent, because transparent button means the player dont have any previous saved data)

if the button animation is finished
we fade out all music channels to 0
and we create and start the Transition object
exact same events as Continue button
but here, we also add the action to delete the Player data storage (so the player start a complete new game from scratch)

If the button animation is finished
then we save all controls data into storage (so if the player has made any change to the controls, it will get saved)
then we set the variable ControlsButtonsOrder to 1 (this variable is used to create the Controls buttons objects in order (refer to (Controls\Pressed) group object))
and we set the ControlsMenu variable back to 14.5 (this variable is used to create the controls buttons objects at the correct Y position and adding 14.5 pixel space between each button (refer to (Controls\Pressed) group object))
and finally, we tween the controls menu scale to 0.01
and we delete all objects related to the controls menu

if the tween (we started in the event above) has finished
we create the main menu buttons again, along with the light objects
and we dellete the objects related to controls menu

events.name: Check if player have saved data or not

events.comment:
We check if the player dont have any saved data into storage
then we set the continue button opacity to 70 (transparent)

If the number of instances of ControlsButtons are = 11 (means all buttons objects has been created)
thenwe wait 0.25 seconds (to wait until the controls button scale in tween has finished)

we check the variable number of the button (we used this condition to pick each instances of the button)
if its 1 (means this is the first button created from the top, and it belongs to MoveRight control (refer to (Controls\Pressed) group event))
then we create the text object on the button position
and we set its text to the variable GameControls.MoveRight (this variable contain the key name)
and we change its X position to center on the button center X
then we set the zorder to 8 (above the button z order)
and finally we link the text and the button (so we can pick each text linked to each button instance)
same as above but for other key controls

whenever this variable is true (this variable is used to udpate all text objects for the controls)
we set the variable to false (to trigger the events below once and for all)
we take into account the text object linked to each control button

we check the variable number of the button (we used this condition to pick each instances of the button)
then we change its text to the control variable GameControls.MoveRight (because the button with variable 1, is the button related to MoveRight key control)
then we center the text object to the button center X position
same as above but for other key controls

if the number of instances of the object PressAKeyWindow is = 1 (means the assign key window object is created)
and the PressAKeyWindow tween is not playing

depending on the number of the pressed button (we stored this value into the variable PressedControlButtonNumber, to know which key the player want to update now)
then we set the MoveRight variable to the last pressed key
and we set the variable UpdateControlsButtonsText to True, so it can update all buttons text object (refer to (Controls\Update buttons text) group event)
then we delete the PressAKeyWindow and fade objects

events.name: Check if other keys have the same key set

events.comment:
here we check if the key the player has assigned is equal to another control that wasalready using the same key
then we set that key control to None (since its key was taken)
same as above but for other keys check
same as above but for other key buttons

events.comment:
same events as "For keyboard keys" above
but here we also allow the player to register Left/right mouse button as a key, but only for the basic attack and float abilitiy

events.name: Check if other keys have the same key set

events.comment:
this events are used to check if the player is hovering on a button, we take into account the linked text object to that button
then we changer the color of that text object to White or black if hovered or not

If mouse left button is pressed
and the number of instances of the object PressAKeyWindow is = 0 (mean its not open/created)
and the cursor is on ControlsButtons
and we take int oaccount the text object linked to the button object
then we create the fade object and PressAKeyWindow object
and we set the variable PressedControlButtonNumber to the pressed control variable NumberInOrderFromTop (this variable is used to know which button instance the player has pressed on)

if the button animation is finished
then we create the fade and ControlsMenu objects
and delete all objects related to main menu
the we wait 0.3 seconds (until the ControlsMenu tween has finished playing)
then we create the back button object

we repeat this event 11 times (beause we want to create the ControlsButtons object 11 time)
and we create the ControlsButtons object at the X position of controls menu and Y position + the variable ControlsButtonsYPosition (this variable is used to add space between each created ControlsButtons object on the Y position)
and we add 21 to the controls menu variable (21 is the space we want to add between each created button on the Y position)
then we set the variable of the ControlsButtons to the variable ControlsButtonsOrder (that start at 1 by default), this variable is used to give a number to each button starting form top to bottom, so in other events we can pick each button instances from this number variable
and finally we add 1 to the variable (so the next created button number variable will be 2, then 3 then 4...etc)
